header_summary:
Antoine, in his correspondence, voices concerns about a proposed adjustment in Bitcoin's transaction validity framework, which aims to refine but not entirely eliminate the "seam" in validity checks. He fears that this tweak may not significantly enhance current proposals and expresses apprehension over a shift towards a "trust-sama" philosophy in Bitcoin development, signaling a move away from its core principle of trust-minimized security. For more details, refer to [Antoine's email](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/8c49d0d1-7f87-4e4c-9636-1e631562bc8dn@googlegroups.com/T/#m7db1ea789c3f1961bdc69a849a8bc027a3dc2ea4).

The clarification in the discussion of Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) reveals that tags such as "todo" and "tbd" in BIP documents are placeholders for future updates, not indicators of unresolved critical issues. This insight is crucial for understanding the actual status and challenges within the Bitcoin development community and helps correct the misconception of significant open issues in formally published BIPs. Detailed information is available at [BIPs clarification](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/06d94f73-eb1e-4072-8135-81f8d38260b9@murch.one/T/#m29879497d2710ade951106c74505a1ebfca71f6a).

Significant changes to BIP127 include removing the *Proof File Format* to focus on more actively used sections, elevating its status from Draft to Complete, indicating a cessation of further modifications. This update underscores the proposal's maturity and readiness for application within the cryptocurrency ecosystem. Engagement and feedback from the community on these revisions are encouraged through the discussion on the GitHub pull request, [BIPs PR 2168](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2168).

The recent updates on the fork-observer instance have reinstated the signet network, now observable at [fork.observer/?network=3](https://fork.observer/?network=3). This tool has captured a two-block reorganization and identified distinct mining contributions, which is pivotal for monitoring and analyzing the network's dynamics. This functionality enhances transparency and aids stakeholders in better understanding the contributions of various network participants.

recent_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
recent_posts.summary:
- Antoine explains a refined transaction validity structure for Bitcoin.
- He doubts the refinement is better than current proposals.
- Antoine criticizes a shift from Bitcoin's trust-minimized model.

recent_posts.title: BIP 3 and issues on BIPs
recent_posts.summary: - The review highlights that BIP issues are mostly minor, not critical unresolved problems.  - "Todo" and "tbd" tags mainly signal placeholders for future additions in BIPs.  - Misunderstandings about open issues arise from unpublished, not published BIP drafts.

recent_posts.title: [BIP127] announcements: removal of Proof File Format and update status to Complete
recent_posts.summary: - BIP127 has removed the *Proof File Format* and updated other parts.  - The status of BIP127 is upgraded from Draft to Complete.  - Community feedback on these BIP127 updates is encouraged.

recent_posts.title: Regular signet reorgs
recent_posts.summary: - The signet network is now accessible via the provided link.  - A two-block reorganization was observed at block height 307073.  - Different miners' coinbase tags, including 'signet-3' associated with your activities, were identified.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Witness Version 3: ML-DSA-65 Post-Quantum Key-Path Spending
recent_posts.summary: - The proposal for a new ML-DSA key commitment output type poses several concerns.  - Adopting ML-DSA-65 over ML-DSA-44 questions the necessity of enhanced security levels.  - Comparisons with SLH-DSA and XMSS suggest potential for more efficient cryptographic alternatives.

recent_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
recent_posts.summary:
- The significance of signature uniqueness flag in cryptocurrency has decreased post-segwit.
- Vulnerabilities in hybrid cryptographic schemes could jeopardize the entire system’s integrity.
- Boris Nagaev suggests a space-saving hybrid scheme integration, needing further evaluation.

active_posts.title: PQC - What is our Goal, Even?
active_posts.summary:
- The PQC community's goal is to secure Bitcoin against quantum threats effectively.
- Popular wallets like Bitcoin.com need focused security enhancements for post-quantum reality.
- Current efforts should avoid complex quantum solutions and prioritize immediate vulnerabilities.

active_posts.title: PQC: Lattice-based signatures
active_posts.summary:
- Nikita's discussion on post-quantum cryptography examines lattice-based signatures.
- Blockstream's focus shift from hash-based to lattice-based methods is debated.
- The community evaluates completeness and impacts of comparative analysis on cryptography.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary:
- A new draft for Testnet5 has been proposed to address Testnet4 issues.
- The draft aims to enhance usability and test BIP54 simultaneously.
- Feedback is requested via GitHub or Bitcoin Development Mailing List.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary:
- BIP-110 version 0.4.1 aims to enhance Bitcoin by limiting transaction data field sizes.
- The v0.4.1 client has stabilized, with specific scriptPubKey and OP_RETURN constraints.
- Implementation of BIP-110 is underway, with broad platform compatibility and community feedback.

active_posts.title: P2WOTS: Post Quantum UTXO Winternitz Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- The revised Bitcoin protocol introduces lightweight Winternitz signatures for UTXO challenges.
- A new Bitcoin output, witness version 2, supports compatibility with existing protocols.
- The proposal enhances post-quantum security, excluding multi-signature configurations.

active_posts.title: QR-based signing flow payloads in Miniscript context
active_posts.summary:
- Recent QR discussions integrate Miniscript, enhancing security in cryptocurrency transactions.
- Proposed QR payloads support Miniscript flows, balancing readability and data efficiency.
- Enhancements aim for secure, user-friendly interactions between wallets and devices.

today_in_history_posts.title: Drivechain -- Request for Discussion
today_in_history_posts.summary: - The Drivechain proposal discusses a hybrid model of federation and miner oversight.  - It includes a configurable delay and a security model similar to Bitcoin's.  - Additional topics include BMM economic models and privacy features like closed seal sets.

today_in_history_posts.title: BOLT 11, real time micro payments, and route redundancy
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The writer queries the absence of a refund address in BOLT 11, unlike BIP 70.
- They discuss adapting a fuel pump for Lightning, noting the need for a refund address.
- Concerns about real-time micropayments' feasibility and automatic failover are raised.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Investment strategies include passive holding and active management of assets.
- Passive investments can lead to economic issues like asset misalignment and underutilization.
- The article considers stabilizing economies using currencies backed by assets like Bitcoin.
